content.claim: Approximately 270 Hungarian schools are currently at risk of closure or consolidation, mostly with under-80 pupils and ~10-pupil classes, with government policy framed as protecting small-settlement schools as long as demand exists while acknowledging mergers will be needed; this primary-school/kindergarten closure wave is the front-line demographic shock, not the selective tracks
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Risk estimate applies to primary schools and kindergartens, not secondary tracks; secondary-specific projections not provided
Government framing emphasizes intent to preserve schools ('as long as demand exists') but does not commit to closure timelines or safeguards
Does not project selective-track enrollment or selectivity ratios under continued cohort decline
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
